beats.narration: Why does an ultrasonic echo happen? Because sound suddenly meets a material that carries it differently. That property is acoustic impedance.

payload.title: WHY ECHOES HAPPEN
payload.sub: Acoustic impedance — the property behind every echo.

beats.narration: Impedance is density times velocity. Steel: forty six and a half megarayls. Water: about one and a half. Air: essentially zero.
Sound meets a boundary. Difference over sum, squared: that's the reflected fraction. Steel to water: eighty eight percent reflects. Steel to air: essentially everything.

payload.heading: How much reflects at a boundary

beats.narration: That mismatch runs the method: couplant displaces the air gap, the back wall mirrors sound home, and gas-filled cracks echo hard. Slag echoes weaker.
The rule: no mismatch, no echo. Every echo on that screen is an impedance change, and why couplant rides in every U T kit.

payload.sub: Why couplant rides in every UT kit.
